title: Chapter Thirty-Eight: Iron and Dharma
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

"Deso -- Desolate Sect?"

"Can't be. Why would they come here?"

"Shaolin's monks were with the magistrate! Even Desolate Sect--"

A bald giant pulled an axe from the table. His eyes gleamed red with drink.

"A youngster like this is Desolate Sect? These lot call themselves brothers!"

He gripped the handle and threw. The sound of air splitting was tremendous.

Hyeon Won-chang did not move. His arms stayed folded. He even smiled.

Yeon-shin drew the Desolate Sword in the blink of an eye and struck.

The axe shattered. Force gathered from his grip and scattered the fragments like shrapnel, a gust of wind rolling through the tavern. Eternal Blossom Fist's second stance -- Advancing Thunder's first move, channelled through steel.

"Fist technique force in a sword strike," Hyeon Won-chang murmured appreciatively.

Yeon-shin stepped forward. The man who had thrown was already waving his hands.

"Wait -- let's talk!"

The Desolate Sword flashed. Pure white light illuminated a face full of despair.

Blood.

The remaining men's complexions changed.

---

The story came quickly after that. Blood winds had swept through the marketplace after the Jeong family's annihilation. A war between black path groups over commercial rights. In the chaos, the Jeong family's administrator had built a merchant group with remarkable skill -- agricultural trade, expanding beyond Xinye County.

But the forests and wealth of the ruined estate were the roots of his enterprise. Black path thugs bribed the magistrate, attacked the merchant group, and dismantled it completely. The remains became a sect called the Xinye White Martial Faction.

"Rotten bastards," Hyeon Won-chang growled.

"I believe you'll handle it well," Ma Jin said. "A day should be enough?"

"It involves his family's land," Hyeon Won-chang said. "If you ask, we can help."

The seniors patted Yeon-shin's head and shoulders and went to their rooms. Ma Jin met Yeon-shin's eyes.

"You've already annihilated the Praying Mantis Sect alone. You're clearly stronger now. Need more hands?"

"I appreciate the thought."

"I'll look into this magistrate when I return. Much will come out."

Hyeon Won-chang was grinning when Yeon-shin turned. "I'll go with you. Having Sir Jeong travel alone? The title of Desolate Master would be looked down upon."

"It's not heroic travel."

"How is sweeping away a black path sect not a hero's deed?"

"Should I add Sir Hyeon's name to the Jeong family's land register?"

"Being a divine hero means not taking payment."

*He seemed frivolous only in Desolate Sect.* Yeon-shin had been thinking it often lately. Hyeon Won-chang was a true hero. The headband on his forehead suited him perfectly.

---

The Xinye White Martial Faction was not far. The main gate was antique and firmly closed -- some local notable's small estate, commandeered.

"Almost a shame to cut it," Hyeon Won-chang said, his Desolate Sword already drawn.

He whirled with a single step. Sharp sword sounds rang as killing intent cut through the gate. Yeon-shin stomped the ground behind him.

Wood fragments scattered in all directions. The wide-open view revealed martial artists holding weapons. They had been prepared.

"Fearless fools came with just two! We'll make you fertiliser for the forests!"

The sect leader -- a large man with an arrogant air. His mouth was still forming the next command when Yeon-shin's charge reached him.

The Radiant Sword Style arose with dim light. It brushed past the sect leader's neck.

Passed by.

The body stood for a moment. Then fell.

---

It was done within the hour. Yeon-shin was gathering documents related to the Jeong family when four people crossed the ruined threshold.

Two monks. A middle-aged man. The magistrate of Xinye County in his official robes. Their gazes swept the groaning black path fighters and the headless sect leader.

"Huh. We're already late," the younger monk said.

"You -- aren't you the Jeong family's child?" The magistrate's cheeks were pale.

"Young master!"

Someone pushed past the stammering official. The middle-aged man's face was bright with recognition.

"You really became a Desolate Sect martial artist! A blue robe! It's like a dream!"

The Jeong family's administrator. The man who had promised to manage the forests. His energy was different now -- clear, weighted, properly trained.

"I heard you haven't been well," Yeon-shin said.

"I lacked strength." A bitter smile. "At this age I became a secular disciple of Shaolin Temple."

*The door inside rattled.* Returning to Xinye. His family's land. The ashes of everything he had known. He kept the door shut, but a crack appeared -- a thin line of light where memory pressed against the seal.

The administrator introduced the old monk beside him.

"This is my master."

"I am Won Jong."

Yellow kasaya. A single palm raised before his chest -- Shaolin's banjang greeting. Wrinkled, watchful eyes.

"Master Won Jong. I am Jeong Yeon-shin of Desolate Sect."

"The young hero's movements were truly amazing! I am Gak Jeong!"

A young warrior monk approached from behind. Handsome. Long-eared. Clear-eyed. An elf-clan monk of Shaolin -- the Little Divine Monk, one of the Eighteen Arhats. Not an ordinary rising talent.

"May I speak with this person, master uncle?"

"Do as you wish. But first." Won Jong stared at Yeon-shin with seemingly bored eyes. "You. Do you have any connection with our temple?"

"This is my first time meeting Shaolin masters."

"Yet your body is mysterious. Well-cultivated, as if you've learned the Muscle Change Classic."

*He sees the Dynamic Technique's results.* Yeon-shin said nothing.

Gak Jeong spoke carefully. "I know your movements are reasonable from a secular viewpoint. But to one who has cultivated only martial arts and Buddhism in a mountain temple, they seem excessive. You will become one of the world's counted masters. This monk cannot help but worry."

"What are you saying, exactly?"

"Martial artists speak through martial arts. I have learned the Iron Sand Palm and Muscle Change Classic among the seventy-two arts. How about a competition in physical achievement? If you agree, I'll give you this."

He opened a wooden box. A sharp herbal scent.

"The Clear Righteousness Pill."

"Clear Righteousness Pill!" Hyeon Won-chang's voice cracked behind them.

A medicine that healed injured energy and blood instantly. Elf-clan secrets combined with the Shaolin Medicine King Hall's arts. They said it let a man fight again even after taking grievous internal injuries.

"If I win," Gak Jeong said, "listen to my dharma lecture for one hour."

"One hour of dharma talk?"

"What -- did you think I'd tell you to shave your head?" The monk grinned as he loosened up. Competitive spirit and Buddhist transcendence woven together in an unusual personality. Not a bad impression.

Yeon-shin accepted the wooden box. Took his stance.

*If we both used internal energy, I'd be far behind him. But in body tempering -- I have cultivated the Dynamic Technique to mastery for years.*

"Shall we begin?"

"Good."

Dazzling fist and leg techniques poured forth. Shaolin's forms -- magnificent, disciplined, deceptively complex. Fists and legs struck Yeon-shin's entire body in all manner of trajectories.

Only his eyes moved by will. He captured each essence.

For twenty exchanges, he made no effective counterattack. Then Gak Jeong crouched low, clothes fluttering with discipline, and rushed forward with two true steps. Dragon Fist among the Shaolin Five Fists.

The fist exploded at the solar plexus. Dust rose. It felt like a strike meant to kill.

Instead, Gak Jeong staggered back two steps. Deep footprints in the dirt.

Yeon-shin stood in the exact same spot. Peaceful face. Unmoved.

"Are you finished, monk?"

---
